$comment: Source of truth for the biomarker catalog. Loaded by `pnpm catalog:seed`.

categories.name: Heart
categories.description: Cholesterol, lipid particles and inflammation markers that help frame cardiovascular risk.

categories.name: Metabolic
categories.description: Blood sugar, insulin signalling and energy-regulation markers tied to metabolic health.

categories.name: Blood
categories.description: Blood count markers that describe oxygen carrying, clotting and cell patterns.

categories.name: Immune Regulation
categories.description: White blood cell and inflammation markers that reflect immune activity and balance.

categories.name: Nutrients
categories.description: Vitamin, mineral and iron markers that help assess nutritional status.

categories.name: Liver
categories.description: Proteins, enzymes and bilirubin markers used to understand liver and biliary patterns.

categories.name: Kidneys
categories.description: Filtration, electrolyte and urine-protein markers that help frame kidney function.

categories.name: Electrolytes
categories.description: Minerals and acid-base markers important for hydration, nerve and muscle function.

categories.name: Thyroid
categories.description: Hormones that help your doctor understand thyroid signalling and activity.

categories.name: Hormones
categories.description: Sex and reproductive hormone markers, grouped so the panel reads the same for any patient.

categories.name: Stress & Aging
categories.description: Hormone markers that can reflect adrenal output and stress physiology over time.

categories.name: Infectious Disease
categories.description: Screening tests for infections that are treatable or worth knowing about early.

categories.name: Cancer Screening
categories.description: Tumour-associated proteins, used for monitoring and clinical follow-up rather than general screening.

categories.name: Urine
categories.description: Urinalysis markers that can show hydration, kidney, infection or metabolic clues.

categories.name: Autoimmunity
categories.description: Immune markers that can point to patterns your doctor may interpret with symptoms and history.

categories.name: Pancreas
categories.description: Digestive enzyme markers that may be interpreted when pancreatic stress is a concern.

categories.name: Environmental Toxins
categories.description: Heavy metal markers interpreted with exposure history and clinical context.

biomarkers.name: Apolipoprotein A1
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures ApoA1, the main protein carried by HDL particles.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It reflects part of the body's reverse cholesterol transport system and may add context to HDL, ApoB and overall cardiovascular risk.
biomarkers.description: Measures ApoA1, the main protein carried by HDL particles.

biomarkers.name: Apolipoprotein B - ApoB
biomarkers.description: Counts the number of atherogenic lipoprotein particles that can enter artery walls; often a sharper cardiovascular risk marker than LDL-C alone.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts the number of atherogenic lipoprotein particles that can enter artery walls; often a sharper cardiovascular risk marker than LDL-C alone.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with cardiovascular disease; atherosclerosis; dyslipidemia; metabolic syndrome; insulin resistance. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Apolipoprotein B-to-A1 Ratio
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Compares atherogenic ApoB-containing particles with ApoA1-containing HDL particles.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: A higher ratio may indicate a less favourable balance between cholesterol-carrying particles linked to plaque formation and those involved in cholesterol removal.
biomarkers.description: Compares atherogenic ApoB-containing particles with ApoA1-containing HDL particles.

biomarkers.name: HDL Cholesterol
biomarkers.description: Measures cholesterol carried by HDL particles, commonly interpreted alongside triglycerides, LDL and other lipid markers for cardiometabolic risk.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures cholesterol carried by HDL particles, commonly interpreted alongside triglycerides, LDL and other lipid markers for cardiometabolic risk.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with low hdl; cardiovascular risk; metabolic syndrome; insulin resistance. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: LDL Cholesterol
biomarkers.description: Measures cholesterol carried in LDL particles, a standard marker used in cardiovascular risk assessment.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures cholesterol carried in LDL particles, a standard marker used in cardiovascular risk assessment.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with high ldl; atherosclerosis; cardiovascular disease; familial hypercholesterolemia. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Non-HDL Cholesterol
biomarkers.description: Captures cholesterol carried by all non-HDL, potentially plaque-forming particles; useful when triglycerides are elevated.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Captures cholesterol carried by all non-HDL, potentially plaque-forming particles; useful when triglycerides are elevated.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with dyslipidemia; cardiovascular disease; metabolic syndrome. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Total Cholesterol
biomarkers.description: Measures the overall amount of cholesterol across lipoprotein particles and is interpreted with HDL, LDL, triglycerides and ApoB.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures the overall amount of cholesterol across lipoprotein particles and is interpreted with HDL, LDL, triglycerides and ApoB.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with dyslipidemia; cardiovascular risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Total Cholesterol / HDL Ratio
biomarkers.description: Compares total cholesterol with HDL and provides a simple lipid risk ratio, though it should be interpreted with ApoB and LDL metrics.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Compares total cholesterol with HDL and provides a simple lipid risk ratio, though it should be interpreted with ApoB and LDL metrics.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with cardiovascular risk; dyslipidemia; metabolic syndrome. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Triglycerides
biomarkers.description: Measures circulating triglyceride-rich fats, which can rise with insulin resistance, alcohol intake, diet, liver stress, or genetics.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures circulating triglyceride-rich fats, which can rise with insulin resistance, alcohol intake, diet, liver stress, or genetics.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with hypertriglyceridemia; metabolic syndrome; diabetes risk; pancreatitis risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Glucose
biomarkers.description: Measures current blood sugar level and helps identify impaired fasting glucose, diabetes risk, or acute glucose dysregulation.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures current blood sugar level and helps identify impaired fasting glucose, diabetes risk, or acute glucose dysregulation.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with prediabetes; diabetes; hypoglycemia; insulin resistance. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Hemoglobin A1c - HbA1c
biomarkers.description: Estimates average blood sugar over roughly the prior two to three months by measuring glycated hemoglobin.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Estimates average blood sugar over roughly the prior two to three months by measuring glycated hemoglobin.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with prediabetes; diabetes; poor glycemic control; insulin resistance. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Insulin
biomarkers.description: The amount of insulin your pancreas is releasing to keep blood glucose in range, measured fasting.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: The amount of insulin your pancreas is releasing to keep blood glucose in range, measured fasting.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: Insulin often rises years before glucose or HbA1c do, so it is an early signal of insulin resistance while it is still reversible.

biomarkers.name: Uric Acid
biomarkers.description: Measures a purine breakdown product; elevated levels can contribute to gout and may track cardiometabolic or kidney risk.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a purine breakdown product; elevated levels can contribute to gout and may track cardiometabolic or kidney risk.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with gout; kidney stones; kidney dysfunction; metabolic syndrome. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Hematocrit
biomarkers.description: Measures the percentage of blood volume made up by red blood cells.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures the percentage of blood volume made up by red blood cells.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with anemia; polycythemia; dehydration. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Hemoglobin
biomarkers.description: Measures the oxygen-carrying protein in red blood cells.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures the oxygen-carrying protein in red blood cells.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with anemia; polycythemia; iron deficiency. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin - MCH
biomarkers.description: The average amount of haemoglobin inside each red blood cell.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: The average amount of haemoglobin inside each red blood cell.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: Low values point toward iron-deficiency patterns and high values toward B12 or folate patterns, helping characterise an anaemia rather than just detect one.

biomarkers.name: Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin Concentration - MCHC
biomarkers.description: How concentrated the haemoglobin is within each red blood cell.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: How concentrated the haemoglobin is within each red blood cell.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It helps distinguish types of anaemia and can flag sample-handling issues, so it is read together with MCV and MCH rather than alone.

biomarkers.name: Mean Corpuscular Volume - MCV
biomarkers.description: Measures average red blood cell size, helping classify anemia patterns.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures average red blood cell size, helping classify anemia patterns.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with iron deficiency anemia; b12/folate deficiency; alcohol/liver disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Peripheral Blood Film
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Examines the size, shape and appearance of blood cells under a microscope.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It can reveal abnormal cell morphology, immature cells or other patterns that help explain anaemia, infection, clotting abnormalities and blood disorders.
biomarkers.description: Examines the size, shape and appearance of blood cells under a microscope.

biomarkers.name: Platelet Count
biomarkers.description: Counts platelets involved in clotting and inflammation.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts platelets involved in clotting and inflammation.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with thrombocytopenia; thrombocytosis; bone marrow/liver/inflammatory disorders. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Red Blood Cell Count
biomarkers.description: Counts red blood cells, interpreted with hemoglobin and hematocrit for oxygen-carrying capacity.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts red blood cells, interpreted with hemoglobin and hematocrit for oxygen-carrying capacity.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with anemia; polycythemia; dehydration. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Red Cell Distribution Width - RDW
biomarkers.description: Measures variation in red blood cell size and can rise early in nutritional anemia or chronic disease patterns.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures variation in red blood cell size and can rise early in nutritional anemia or chronic disease patterns.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with iron deficiency; b12/folate deficiency; anemia; inflammation. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Basophils
biomarkers.description: Counts a white blood cell type involved in allergic and inflammatory responses.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts a white blood cell type involved in allergic and inflammatory responses.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with allergic disease; chronic inflammation; myeloproliferative disorders. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Eosinophils
biomarkers.description: Counts white blood cells associated with allergy, asthma, parasites and certain inflammatory disorders.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts white blood cells associated with allergy, asthma, parasites and certain inflammatory disorders.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with allergies; asthma; parasitic infection; eosinophilic disorders. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures how quickly red blood cells settle in a tube over one hour, an indirect sign of inflammation.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: A faster rate can occur with inflammation, infection, autoimmune disease or other conditions. It is nonspecific and should be interpreted with symptoms and other markers.
biomarkers.description: Measures how quickly red blood cells settle in a tube over one hour, an indirect sign of inflammation.

biomarkers.name: High-Sensitivity C-Reactive Protein - hs-CRP
biomarkers.description: Measures low-grade systemic inflammation and is often used with lipid markers to refine cardiovascular and inflammatory risk.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures low-grade systemic inflammation and is often used with lipid markers to refine cardiovascular and inflammatory risk.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with systemic inflammation; cardiovascular risk; infection/inflammatory states; metabolic syndrome. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Lymphocytes
biomarkers.description: Counts immune cells central to viral defense and adaptive immunity.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts immune cells central to viral defense and adaptive immunity.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with viral infection; immune deficiency; lymphoproliferative disorders. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Monocytes
biomarkers.description: Counts immune cells involved in cleanup, chronic inflammation and infection response.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts immune cells involved in cleanup, chronic inflammation and infection response.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with chronic inflammation; infection; autoimmune disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Neutrophils
biomarkers.description: Counts frontline white blood cells that often rise with bacterial infection or acute inflammation.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts frontline white blood cells that often rise with bacterial infection or acute inflammation.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with bacterial infection; acute inflammation; bone marrow stress. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: White Blood Cell Count
biomarkers.description: Measures total white blood cells as a broad screen for infection, inflammation and marrow response.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures total white blood cells as a broad screen for infection, inflammation and marrow response.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with infection; inflammation; leukopenia/leukocytosis; bone marrow disorders. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Calcium
biomarkers.description: Measures blood calcium, important for bone, parathyroid, kidney, nerve and muscle function.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures blood calcium, important for bone, parathyroid, kidney, nerve and muscle function.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with hypercalcemia; hypocalcemia; parathyroid disorders; kidney disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Corrected Calcium
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Estimates blood calcium after adjusting total calcium for the albumin level.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It can provide a more useful estimate of calcium status when albumin is abnormal, although ionised calcium may be more accurate in some clinical situations.
biomarkers.description: Estimates blood calcium after adjusting total calcium for the albumin level.

biomarkers.name: Ferritin
biomarkers.description: Reflects stored iron and can also rise with inflammation or liver stress.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Reflects stored iron and can also rise with inflammation or liver stress.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with iron deficiency; iron overload; inflammation; liver disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Homocysteine
biomarkers.description: Measures an amino acid linked to methylation, B-vitamin status and vascular risk when elevated.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures an amino acid linked to methylation, B-vitamin status and vascular risk when elevated.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with b12/folate deficiency; cardiovascular risk; methylation issues. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Iron
biomarkers.description: Measures circulating iron available for red blood cell production and other functions.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures circulating iron available for red blood cell production and other functions.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with iron deficiency; iron overload; anemia. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Iron % Saturation
biomarkers.description: Shows the percentage of transferrin iron-binding sites that are occupied by iron.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Shows the percentage of transferrin iron-binding sites that are occupied by iron.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with iron deficiency; hemochromatosis; anemia. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Iron Binding Capacity
biomarkers.description: Measures the blood's capacity to bind iron and helps distinguish iron deficiency from inflammation-related patterns.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures the blood's capacity to bind iron and helps distinguish iron deficiency from inflammation-related patterns.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with iron deficiency; anemia of inflammation; iron overload. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Magnesium
biomarkers.description: Measures magnesium status relevant to muscle, nerve, glucose, blood pressure and energy metabolism.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures magnesium status relevant to muscle, nerve, glucose, blood pressure and energy metabolism.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with magnesium deficiency; arrhythmia risk; muscle cramps; metabolic dysfunction. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Phosphorus
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures phosphate in the blood, a mineral involved in bones, energy production and cell function.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: Abnormal levels can reflect kidney, parathyroid, vitamin D, nutritional or bone-related problems. Normal ranges vary substantially by age.
biomarkers.description: Measures phosphate in the blood, a mineral involved in bones, energy production and cell function.

biomarkers.name: Transferrin
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures the main protein that transports iron through the bloodstream.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It helps assess iron availability and nutritional status. Levels can also change with inflammation, liver disease, pregnancy and iron deficiency.
biomarkers.description: Measures the main protein that transports iron through the bloodstream.

biomarkers.name: Vitamin D
biomarkers.description: Measures vitamin D status, important for bone, immune, muscle and metabolic health.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures vitamin D status, important for bone, immune, muscle and metabolic health.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with vitamin d deficiency; bone loss; immune dysfunction. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Alanine Transaminase - ALT
biomarkers.description: A liver enzyme that rises when liver cells are irritated or damaged.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: A liver enzyme that rises when liver cells are irritated or damaged.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with fatty liver disease; hepatitis; liver injury. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Albumin
biomarkers.description: Measures the main blood protein made by the liver; low levels can reflect liver, kidney, inflammation or nutrition issues.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures the main blood protein made by the liver; low levels can reflect liver, kidney, inflammation or nutrition issues.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with liver disease; kidney protein loss; malnutrition; inflammation. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Albumin / Globulin Ratio
biomarkers.description: Compares albumin and globulin levels to flag shifts in liver production, immune proteins or protein loss.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Compares albumin and globulin levels to flag shifts in liver production, immune proteins or protein loss.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with liver disease; chronic inflammation; immune disorders; kidney disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Alkaline Phosphatase - ALP
biomarkers.description: An enzyme from bile ducts and bone; elevations can reflect cholestasis or increased bone turnover.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: An enzyme from bile ducts and bone; elevations can reflect cholestasis or increased bone turnover.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with bile duct disease; liver disease; bone disorders. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Aspartate Aminotransferase - AST
biomarkers.description: An enzyme found in liver and muscle; elevations can reflect liver injury, muscle injury, or alcohol-related stress.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: An enzyme found in liver and muscle; elevations can reflect liver injury, muscle injury, or alcohol-related stress.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with liver injury; muscle injury; alcohol-related liver disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Fibrosis-4 (FIB-4) Score
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Combines age, AST, ALT and platelet count to estimate the likelihood of advanced liver fibrosis.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It helps identify who may need further liver assessment. Interpretation changes with age and the score is less reliable in younger adults.
biomarkers.description: Combines age, AST, ALT and platelet count to estimate the likelihood of advanced liver fibrosis.

biomarkers.name: Gamma-Glutamyl Transferase - GGT
biomarkers.description: A liver and bile duct enzyme often sensitive to alcohol exposure, fatty liver and cholestasis.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: A liver and bile duct enzyme often sensitive to alcohol exposure, fatty liver and cholestasis.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with fatty liver disease; alcohol-related liver stress; bile duct disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Globulin
biomarkers.description: Measures a group of proteins that include antibodies; abnormal levels can reflect immune, liver, or inflammatory changes.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a group of proteins that include antibodies; abnormal levels can reflect immune, liver, or inflammatory changes.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with chronic inflammation; liver disease; immune disorders; monoclonal gammopathy. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Total Bilirubin
biomarkers.description: Measures bilirubin from red blood cell breakdown and liver/bile processing.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures bilirubin from red blood cell breakdown and liver/bile processing.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with gilbert syndrome; liver disease; bile obstruction; hemolysis. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Total Protein
biomarkers.description: The combined albumin and globulin in your blood.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: The combined albumin and globulin in your blood.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It gives a broad read on nutrition, liver synthesis and immune activity, and is interpreted alongside the albumin-to-globulin ratio rather than on its own.

biomarkers.name: Albumin / Microalbumin - Urine
biomarkers.description: Measures albumin protein in urine, an early sign of kidney or vascular damage when persistent.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures albumin protein in urine, an early sign of kidney or vascular damage when persistent.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with chronic kidney disease; diabetes-related kidney disease; hypertension-related kidney damage. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Creatinine
biomarkers.description: Measures a muscle metabolism waste product used to estimate kidney filtration.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a muscle metabolism waste product used to estimate kidney filtration.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with kidney dysfunction; chronic kidney disease; acute kidney injury. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate - eGFR
biomarkers.description: Estimates kidney filtration capacity from creatinine and demographic factors.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Estimates kidney filtration capacity from creatinine and demographic factors.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with chronic kidney disease; acute kidney dysfunction; diabetes-related kidney disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Urea
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a waste product formed when the body breaks down protein and cleared mainly by the kidneys.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It can provide context on kidney function, hydration and protein metabolism when interpreted alongside creatinine and estimated GFR.
biomarkers.description: Measures a waste product formed when the body breaks down protein and cleared mainly by the kidneys.

biomarkers.name: Urine Albumin-to-Creatinine Ratio
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Compares urine albumin with urine creatinine to estimate abnormal protein leakage from the kidneys.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: An elevated ratio can be an early sign of kidney damage, including damage associated with diabetes or high blood pressure.
biomarkers.description: Compares urine albumin with urine creatinine to estimate abnormal protein leakage from the kidneys.

biomarkers.name: Urine Creatinine
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures creatinine concentration in a urine sample.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It mainly helps assess sample concentration and calculate ratios such as the urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio. It is usually not interpreted as a standalone health target.
biomarkers.description: Measures creatinine concentration in a urine sample.

biomarkers.name: Chloride
biomarkers.description: Measures a major electrolyte involved in acid-base balance and fluid regulation.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a major electrolyte involved in acid-base balance and fluid regulation.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with dehydration; kidney dysfunction; acid-base disorders. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Potassium
biomarkers.description: Measures a key electrolyte for heart rhythm, muscle and nerve function.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a key electrolyte for heart rhythm, muscle and nerve function.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with hyperkalemia; hypokalemia; kidney dysfunction; arrhythmia risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Sodium
biomarkers.description: Measures a major electrolyte that reflects fluid balance and neurologic risk when abnormal.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a major electrolyte that reflects fluid balance and neurologic risk when abnormal.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with hyponatremia; hypernatremia; dehydration; kidney/adrenal disorders. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Free Thyroxine - Free T4
biomarkers.description: Measures the main circulating thyroid hormone available for conversion to T3; interpreted with TSH and Free T3.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures the main circulating thyroid hormone available for conversion to T3; interpreted with TSH and Free T3.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with hypothyroidism; hyperthyroidism; thyroid dysfunction. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Free Triiodothyronine - Free T3
biomarkers.description: Measures the active thyroid hormone available to tissues and helps interpret thyroid conversion and hyperthyroid patterns.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures the active thyroid hormone available to tissues and helps interpret thyroid conversion and hyperthyroid patterns.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with hyperthyroidism; thyroid conversion issues; thyroid dysfunction. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone - TSH
biomarkers.description: Measures pituitary signaling to the thyroid and is the primary screening marker for thyroid under- or over-activity.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures pituitary signaling to the thyroid and is the primary screening marker for thyroid under- or over-activity.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with hypothyroidism; hyperthyroidism; thyroid dysfunction. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: DHEA-Sulfate
biomarkers.description: Measures an adrenal androgen precursor that can reflect adrenal output and androgen balance.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures an adrenal androgen precursor that can reflect adrenal output and androgen balance.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with adrenal dysfunction; pcos; androgen excess; aging-related hormonal change. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Estradiol
biomarkers.description: Measures a primary estrogen important for reproductive, bone, brain and cardiometabolic health.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a primary estrogen important for reproductive, bone, brain and cardiometabolic health.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with menopause transition; estrogen deficiency/excess; fertility issues; pcos. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Follicle Stimulating Hormone - FSH
biomarkers.description: Measures pituitary signaling to the ovaries or testes; useful for fertility, ovarian reserve, menopause and gonadal function.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures pituitary signaling to the ovaries or testes; useful for fertility, ovarian reserve, menopause and gonadal function.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with menopause transition; ovarian insufficiency; hypogonadism; fertility issues. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Free Androgen Index
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Calculates an estimate of available androgen using total testosterone and sex hormone-binding globulin.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It can support assessment of androgen excess or deficiency in selected patients, but its usefulness depends on sex, assay method and clinical context.
biomarkers.description: Calculates an estimate of available androgen using total testosterone and sex hormone-binding globulin.

biomarkers.name: Free Testosterone
biomarkers.description: Measures the unbound fraction of testosterone available to tissues.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures the unbound fraction of testosterone available to tissues.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with low testosterone; androgen excess; pcos; hypogonadism. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Luteinizing Hormone - LH
biomarkers.description: Measures pituitary signaling involved in ovulation and testosterone production.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures pituitary signaling involved in ovulation and testosterone production.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with pcos; ovulatory dysfunction; hypogonadism; menopause transition. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Progesterone
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures progesterone, a hormone involved in ovulation, the menstrual cycle and pregnancy.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: Interpretation depends heavily on sex, age, menstrual-cycle timing, pregnancy status and hormone therapy.
biomarkers.description: Measures progesterone, a hormone involved in ovulation, the menstrual cycle and pregnancy.

biomarkers.name: Prolactin
biomarkers.description: Measures a pituitary hormone involved in lactation and reproductive signaling; elevations can disrupt cycles or testosterone.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a pituitary hormone involved in lactation and reproductive signaling; elevations can disrupt cycles or testosterone.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with hyperprolactinemia; pituitary adenoma; infertility; hypogonadism. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Prostate Specific Antigen - PSA Total
biomarkers.description: Measures total prostate-specific antigen, a prostate marker that can rise with benign enlargement, inflammation, or cancer.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures total prostate-specific antigen, a prostate marker that can rise with benign enlargement, inflammation, or cancer.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia; prostatitis; prostate cancer risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Sex Hormone Binding Globulin - SHBG
biomarkers.description: Measures the protein that binds sex hormones and affects how much testosterone or estradiol is bioavailable.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures the protein that binds sex hormones and affects how much testosterone or estradiol is bioavailable.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with hormone imbalance; insulin resistance; pcos; liver/thyroid influences. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Total Testosterone
biomarkers.description: Measures total circulating testosterone, including bound and free fractions.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures total circulating testosterone, including bound and free fractions.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with low testosterone; androgen excess; hypogonadism; pcos. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Cortisol
biomarkers.description: Measures a snapshot of cortisol, the main stress hormone produced by the adrenal glands.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a snapshot of cortisol, the main stress hormone produced by the adrenal glands.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with adrenal dysfunction; cushing syndrome; addison disease; stress physiology. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Insulin-Like Growth Factor 1
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures insulin-like growth factor 1, a hormone that reflects average growth-hormone activity.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It is used when evaluating growth-hormone excess or deficiency. Levels naturally vary with age, sex, nutrition and liver function.
biomarkers.description: Measures insulin-like growth factor 1, a hormone that reflects average growth-hormone activity.

biomarkers.name: Helicobacter pylori IgG Antibody
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects IgG antibodies against Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium associated with stomach inflammation and ulcers.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: A reactive result indicates exposure but does not reliably prove active infection. Breath or stool testing may be needed to confirm current infection.
biomarkers.description: Detects IgG antibodies against Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium associated with stomach inflammation and ulcers.

biomarkers.name: Hepatitis A Antibody
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects antibodies to the hepatitis A virus.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: Depending on the antibody type, a reactive result may indicate immunity from vaccination or past infection, while IgM antibodies can suggest recent infection.
biomarkers.description: Detects antibodies to the hepatitis A virus.

biomarkers.name: Hepatitis B Surface Antibody
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures antibodies against the hepatitis B surface antigen.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It helps determine whether there is protective immunity from vaccination or previous infection. Interpretation depends on vaccination history and other hepatitis B markers.
biomarkers.description: Measures antibodies against the hepatitis B surface antigen.

biomarkers.name: Hepatitis B Surface Antigen
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects hepatitis B surface antigen, a marker that the hepatitis B virus may be present.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: A reactive result requires further testing to determine whether infection is acute, chronic or otherwise clinically significant.
biomarkers.description: Detects hepatitis B surface antigen, a marker that the hepatitis B virus may be present.

biomarkers.name: HIV-1/2 Antigen and Antibody Screen
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Screens for HIV-1 and HIV-2 antibodies together with the HIV p24 antigen.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It can detect HIV earlier than antibody-only testing. A reactive screening result must be confirmed with follow-up testing.
biomarkers.description: Screens for HIV-1 and HIV-2 antibodies together with the HIV p24 antigen.

biomarkers.name: Treponema pallidum Antibody
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects antibodies against Treponema pallidum, the bacterium that causes syphilis.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: A reactive result may reflect current or previously treated infection and requires interpretation with confirmatory tests and clinical history.
biomarkers.description: Detects antibodies against Treponema pallidum, the bacterium that causes syphilis.

biomarkers.name: Alpha-Fetoprotein
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures alpha-fetoprotein, a protein that may rise in certain liver and germ-cell conditions.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It is mainly used with other tests for monitoring or diagnostic evaluation. An elevated result does not by itself diagnose cancer.
biomarkers.description: Measures alpha-fetoprotein, a protein that may rise in certain liver and germ-cell conditions.

biomarkers.name: Cancer Antigen 19-9
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures cancer antigen 19-9, a marker associated with pancreatic and biliary conditions.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It may help monitor selected cancers or investigate symptoms, but benign conditions can also raise it and it should not be used alone for diagnosis.
biomarkers.description: Measures cancer antigen 19-9, a marker associated with pancreatic and biliary conditions.

biomarkers.name: Carcinoembryonic Antigen
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures carcinoembryonic antigen, a protein that can rise in some cancers and several non-cancerous conditions.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: It is most useful for monitoring selected cancers over time rather than general cancer screening. Smoking and inflammation can affect the result.
biomarkers.description: Measures carcinoembryonic antigen, a protein that can rise in some cancers and several non-cancerous conditions.

biomarkers.name: Other Urine Microscopy Findings
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Captures other cells, organisms, casts or material reported during microscopic urine examination.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: The significance depends on the exact finding and clinical context. Unexpected or significant findings may require clinician review or additional testing.
biomarkers.description: Captures other cells, organisms, casts or material reported during microscopic urine examination.

biomarkers.name: Squamous Epithelial Cells
biomarkers.description: Counts skin/genital tract cells in urine; high levels often indicate sample contamination.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts skin/genital tract cells in urine; high levels often indicate sample contamination.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with sample contamination; urinary tract inflammation context. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Appearance, Urine
biomarkers.description: Describes urine clarity; cloudiness can reflect cells, crystals, bacteria or mucus.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Describes urine clarity; cloudiness can reflect cells, crystals, bacteria or mucus.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with uti; crystalluria; dehydration; kidney/urinary inflammation. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Bilirubin, Urine
biomarkers.description: Detects bilirubin in urine, which may signal liver or bile duct problems.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects bilirubin in urine, which may signal liver or bile duct problems.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with liver disease; bile duct obstruction; hepatitis. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Color, Urine
biomarkers.description: Describes urine color, influenced by hydration, medications, blood, bilirubin and diet.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Describes urine color, influenced by hydration, medications, blood, bilirubin and diet.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with dehydration; hematuria; liver/bile disease; medication/diet effects. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Urine Crystals
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Identifies crystal types seen in urine under a microscope.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: Some crystals are harmless, while persistent or specific crystal types may indicate dehydration, metabolic conditions or increased kidney-stone risk.
biomarkers.description: Identifies crystal types seen in urine under a microscope.

biomarkers.name: Glucose, Urine
biomarkers.description: Detects glucose spilling into urine, usually when blood glucose is high or renal glucose handling is altered.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects glucose spilling into urine, usually when blood glucose is high or renal glucose handling is altered.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with diabetes; hyperglycemia; renal glycosuria. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Hyaline Casts, Urine
biomarkers.description: Reports casts formed in kidney tubules; small numbers can occur with dehydration or exercise, higher levels may indicate kidney stress.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Reports casts formed in kidney tubules; small numbers can occur with dehydration or exercise, higher levels may indicate kidney stress.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with dehydration; kidney disease; exercise-related changes. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Ketones, Urine
biomarkers.description: Detects ketones from fat metabolism, fasting, low-carbohydrate intake, or uncontrolled diabetes.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects ketones from fat metabolism, fasting, low-carbohydrate intake, or uncontrolled diabetes.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with diabetic ketoacidosis risk; fasting/ketosis; poor glycemic control. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Leukocyte Esterase, Urine
biomarkers.description: Screens for white blood cell activity in urine, often used in UTI assessment.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Screens for white blood cell activity in urine, often used in UTI assessment.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with urinary tract infection; urinary inflammation. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Nitrite, Urine
biomarkers.description: Detects nitrite-producing bacteria in urine, supporting UTI diagnosis when positive.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects nitrite-producing bacteria in urine, supporting UTI diagnosis when positive.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with urinary tract infection. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Occult Blood, Urine
biomarkers.description: Detects blood or hemoglobin in urine and should be interpreted with microscopy and clinical context.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects blood or hemoglobin in urine and should be interpreted with microscopy and clinical context.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with hematuria; kidney stones; uti; kidney disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: pH, Urine
biomarkers.description: Measures urine acidity/alkalinity, influenced by diet, infection, kidney handling and stone risk.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures urine acidity/alkalinity, influenced by diet, infection, kidney handling and stone risk.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with kidney stone risk; uti; renal tubular acidosis. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Protein, Urine
biomarkers.description: Screens for protein leakage into urine, a marker of kidney or urinary tract pathology when persistent.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Screens for protein leakage into urine, a marker of kidney or urinary tract pathology when persistent.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with kidney disease; diabetes-related kidney disease; hypertension-related kidney damage. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Red Blood Cell, Urine
biomarkers.description: Counts red cells in urine microscopy, helping confirm hematuria.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts red cells in urine microscopy, helping confirm hematuria.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with kidney stones; uti; kidney disease; urinary tract bleeding. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Specific Gravity, Urine
biomarkers.description: Measures urine concentration and hydration status.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures urine concentration and hydration status.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with dehydration; overhydration; kidney concentrating defects. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Urine Urobilinogen
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Assesses urobilinogen in urine, a breakdown product formed from bilirubin.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: Abnormal results can provide clues about liver function, bile flow or increased red-cell breakdown when interpreted with urine bilirubin and blood tests.
biomarkers.description: Assesses urobilinogen in urine, a breakdown product formed from bilirubin.

biomarkers.name: White Blood Cell, Urine
biomarkers.description: Counts white cells in urine, supporting infection or urinary tract inflammation assessment.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts white cells in urine, supporting infection or urinary tract inflammation assessment.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with urinary tract infection; kidney/urinary inflammation. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: HDL Large
biomarkers.description: Estimates larger HDL particles, one part of advanced lipid profiling that may reflect reverse cholesterol transport and cardiometabolic status.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Estimates larger HDL particles, one part of advanced lipid profiling that may reflect reverse cholesterol transport and cardiometabolic status.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with cardiovascular risk; dyslipidemia; metabolic syndrome. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: LDL Medium
biomarkers.description: Measures medium-sized LDL particles as part of advanced lipid subfraction testing.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures medium-sized LDL particles as part of advanced lipid subfraction testing.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with dyslipidemia; atherosclerosis; cardiovascular risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: LDL Particle Number
biomarkers.description: Counts LDL particles rather than cholesterol mass; higher particle counts generally mean more opportunities for particles to enter vessel walls.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts LDL particles rather than cholesterol mass; higher particle counts generally mean more opportunities for particles to enter vessel walls.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with atherosclerosis; cardiovascular disease; dyslipidemia; metabolic syndrome. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: LDL Pattern
biomarkers.description: Classifies LDL particle pattern, often distinguishing smaller, denser LDL patterns from larger, more buoyant patterns.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Classifies LDL particle pattern, often distinguishing smaller, denser LDL patterns from larger, more buoyant patterns.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with atherogenic dyslipidemia; metabolic syndrome; cardiovascular risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: LDL Peak Size
biomarkers.description: Reports the dominant LDL particle size; smaller peak sizes can accompany insulin resistance and a more atherogenic lipid pattern.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Reports the dominant LDL particle size; smaller peak sizes can accompany insulin resistance and a more atherogenic lipid pattern.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with atherogenic dyslipidemia; insulin resistance; cardiovascular risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: LDL Small
biomarkers.description: Measures small dense LDL particles, a pattern associated with insulin resistance and higher cardiovascular risk.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures small dense LDL particles, a pattern associated with insulin resistance and higher cardiovascular risk.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with atherogenic dyslipidemia; insulin resistance; metabolic syndrome; cardiovascular risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Lipoprotein(a)
biomarkers.description: Measures a largely inherited cholesterol-carrying particle that can increase lifetime plaque and clotting risk independent of standard cholesterol.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a largely inherited cholesterol-carrying particle that can increase lifetime plaque and clotting risk independent of standard cholesterol.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with cardiovascular disease; atherosclerosis; aortic valve disease; familial lipid risk; stroke risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Leptin
biomarkers.description: Measures a hormone involved in appetite and energy regulation; high levels can suggest leptin resistance or excess adiposity.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures a hormone involved in appetite and energy regulation; high levels can suggest leptin resistance or excess adiposity.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with obesity; leptin resistance; metabolic syndrome; insulin resistance. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: ABO Group and Rhesus - Rh Factor
aliases: ABO Group and Rhesus - Rh Factor
Rh Factor ABO Group and Rhesus
biomarkers.description: Identifies ABO blood type, useful for transfusion compatibility and some risk stratification contexts.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Identifies ABO blood type, useful for transfusion compatibility and some risk stratification contexts.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with transfusion compatibility; blood type-related risk context. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Mean Platelet Volume - MPV
biomarkers.description: Measures average platelet size, which can reflect platelet production and activation patterns.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures average platelet size, which can reflect platelet production and activation patterns.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with platelet production disorders; inflammation; thrombosis risk context. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Arachidonic Acid / EPA Ratio
biomarkers.description: Compares pro-inflammatory omega-6 arachidonic acid with anti-inflammatory omega-3 EPA, indicating fatty acid balance.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Compares pro-inflammatory omega-6 arachidonic acid with anti-inflammatory omega-3 EPA, indicating fatty acid balance.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with inflammatory balance; cardiovascular risk; low omega-3 status. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Methylmalonic Acid
biomarkers.description: A functional marker of vitamin B12 status; elevations can appear before serum B12 is clearly low.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: A functional marker of vitamin B12 status; elevations can appear before serum B12 is clearly low.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with vitamin b12 deficiency; neuropathy risk; anemia. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Omega-3 DHA
biomarkers.description: Measures docosahexaenoic acid, an omega-3 important for brain, retina and cell membrane health.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures docosahexaenoic acid, an omega-3 important for brain, retina and cell membrane health.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with low omega-3 status; neurocognitive health risk; cardiovascular risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Omega-3 DPA
biomarkers.description: Measures docosapentaenoic acid, an intermediate omega-3 fatty acid related to EPA and DHA metabolism.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures docosapentaenoic acid, an intermediate omega-3 fatty acid related to EPA and DHA metabolism.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with low omega-3 status; cardiovascular risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Omega-3 EPA
biomarkers.description: Measures eicosapentaenoic acid, an omega-3 linked to triglyceride and inflammatory balance.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures eicosapentaenoic acid, an omega-3 linked to triglyceride and inflammatory balance.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with low omega-3 status; hypertriglyceridemia; inflammatory imbalance. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Omega-3 Total
biomarkers.description: Measures total omega-3 fatty acid status, often used to assess dietary intake and cardiovascular-inflammatory balance.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures total omega-3 fatty acid status, often used to assess dietary intake and cardiovascular-inflammatory balance.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with low omega-3 status; cardiovascular risk; inflammatory imbalance. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Omega-6 / Omega-3 Ratio
biomarkers.description: Compares omega-6 to omega-3 fatty acids; a high ratio can suggest an imbalance toward pro-inflammatory fatty acids.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Compares omega-6 to omega-3 fatty acids; a high ratio can suggest an imbalance toward pro-inflammatory fatty acids.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with inflammatory imbalance; cardiovascular risk; low omega-3 status. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Omega-6 Arachidonic Acid
biomarkers.description: Measures arachidonic acid, an omega-6 fatty acid involved in inflammatory signaling.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures arachidonic acid, an omega-6 fatty acid involved in inflammatory signaling.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with inflammatory balance; cardiovascular risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Omega-6 Linoleic Acid
biomarkers.description: Measures linoleic acid, an essential omega-6 fatty acid from diet.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures linoleic acid, an essential omega-6 fatty acid from diet.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with fatty acid imbalance; essential fatty acid status. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Omega-6 Total
biomarkers.description: Measures total omega-6 fatty acid status, interpreted alongside omega-3 levels and ratios.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures total omega-6 fatty acid status, interpreted alongside omega-3 levels and ratios.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with fatty acid imbalance; inflammatory balance. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Zinc
biomarkers.description: Measures zinc status, which supports immunity, wound healing, hormones and enzyme function.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures zinc status, which supports immunity, wound healing, hormones and enzyme function.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with zinc deficiency; immune dysfunction; poor wound healing. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Blood Urea Nitrogen - BUN
biomarkers.description: Measures urea nitrogen from protein metabolism, affected by kidney filtration, hydration and protein intake.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures urea nitrogen from protein metabolism, affected by kidney filtration, hydration and protein intake.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with kidney dysfunction; dehydration; high protein catabolism. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: BUN / Creatinine Ratio
biomarkers.description: Compares BUN with creatinine to help interpret hydration, kidney perfusion and kidney function patterns.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Compares BUN with creatinine to help interpret hydration, kidney perfusion and kidney function patterns.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with dehydration; kidney dysfunction; gastrointestinal bleeding. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Carbon Dioxide
biomarkers.description: Reflects bicarbonate level and acid-base balance in routine chemistry panels.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Reflects bicarbonate level and acid-base balance in routine chemistry panels.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with metabolic acidosis; metabolic alkalosis; kidney/lung disorders. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Thyroglobulin Antibodies - TgAb
biomarkers.description: Detects antibodies against thyroglobulin, often used to assess autoimmune thyroid activity.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects antibodies against thyroglobulin, often used to assess autoimmune thyroid activity.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with hashimoto thyroiditis; graves disease; autoimmune thyroid disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Amorphous Sediment, Urine
biomarkers.description: Reports non-specific crystals or sediment seen in urine microscopy.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Reports non-specific crystals or sediment seen in urine microscopy.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with urine concentration changes; crystalluria; kidney stone risk context. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Bacteria, Urine
biomarkers.description: Detects bacteria on urine microscopy, interpreted with symptoms and leukocyte/nitrite markers.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects bacteria on urine microscopy, interpreted with symptoms and leukocyte/nitrite markers.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with urinary tract infection; contamination. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Calcium Oxalate Crystals, Urine
biomarkers.description: Detects calcium oxalate crystals, which can be associated with kidney stone risk.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects calcium oxalate crystals, which can be associated with kidney stone risk.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with kidney stones; hyperoxaluria; dehydration. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Leukocyte, Urine
biomarkers.description: Counts white cells in urine, supporting infection or urinary tract inflammation assessment.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Counts white cells in urine, supporting infection or urinary tract inflammation assessment.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with urinary tract infection; kidney/urinary inflammation. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Yeast, Urine
biomarkers.description: Detects yeast in urine microscopy, which may reflect contamination or fungal urinary infection in susceptible people.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects yeast in urine microscopy, which may reflect contamination or fungal urinary infection in susceptible people.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with yeast contamination; fungal uti; diabetes-related infection risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Amylase
biomarkers.description: A digestive enzyme from pancreas and salivary glands; elevations can occur with pancreatic or salivary inflammation.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: A digestive enzyme from pancreas and salivary glands; elevations can occur with pancreatic or salivary inflammation.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with pancreatitis; salivary gland disease; pancreatic stress. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Anti-Mullerian Hormone - AMH
biomarkers.description: Reflects ovarian reserve and is commonly used in fertility and reproductive planning contexts.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Reflects ovarian reserve and is commonly used in fertility and reproductive planning contexts.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with low ovarian reserve; pcos; fertility assessment. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Antinuclear Antibodies - ANA Pattern
biomarkers.description: Describes the staining pattern of ANA antibodies and can guide follow-up autoimmune testing.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Describes the staining pattern of ANA antibodies and can guide follow-up autoimmune testing.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with systemic lupus erythematosus; sjogren syndrome; scleroderma; mixed connective tissue disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Antinuclear Antibodies - ANA Screen
biomarkers.description: Screens for antinuclear antibodies that may appear in systemic autoimmune conditions, though positives can occur without disease.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Screens for antinuclear antibodies that may appear in systemic autoimmune conditions, though positives can occur without disease.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with lupus; sjogren syndrome; scleroderma; autoimmune hepatitis; thyroid autoimmunity. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Antinuclear Antibodies Titer
biomarkers.description: Quantifies ANA antibody dilution level; higher titers are more suggestive but still require clinical context.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Quantifies ANA antibody dilution level; higher titers are more suggestive but still require clinical context.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with systemic autoimmune disease; lupus; sjogren syndrome; scleroderma. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Lead
biomarkers.description: Measures lead exposure, a toxic heavy metal affecting neurologic, blood, kidney and cardiovascular systems.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures lead exposure, a toxic heavy metal affecting neurologic, blood, kidney and cardiovascular systems.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with lead toxicity; anemia; neurologic toxicity; kidney dysfunction. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Lipase
biomarkers.description: A pancreatic enzyme that rises more specifically with pancreatic inflammation.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: A pancreatic enzyme that rises more specifically with pancreatic inflammation.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with pancreatitis; pancreatic injury. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Mercury
biomarkers.description: Measures mercury exposure, often associated with seafood or environmental exposure.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures mercury exposure, often associated with seafood or environmental exposure.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with mercury toxicity; neurologic symptoms; kidney effects. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Prostate Specific Antigen - PSA % Free
biomarkers.description: Calculates free PSA as a percentage of total PSA to help distinguish benign enlargement from higher-risk prostate patterns.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Calculates free PSA as a percentage of total PSA to help distinguish benign enlargement from higher-risk prostate patterns.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia; prostate cancer risk; prostatitis. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Prostate Specific Antigen - PSA Free
biomarkers.description: Measures unbound prostate-specific antigen; interpreted with total PSA to refine prostate risk assessment.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Measures unbound prostate-specific antigen; interpreted with total PSA to refine prostate risk assessment.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia; prostatitis; prostate cancer risk. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.

biomarkers.name: Rheumatoid Factor - RF
biomarkers.description: Detects an autoantibody commonly associated with rheumatoid arthritis but also seen in other inflammatory or infectious states.
biomarkers.whatItMeasures: Detects an autoantibody commonly associated with rheumatoid arthritis but also seen in other inflammatory or infectious states.
biomarkers.whyItMatters: This can be associated with rheumatoid arthritis; sjogren syndrome; chronic infection; autoimmune disease. Your doctor may interpret this alongside other markers, symptoms, medications, and history.
